name: KrillApp.Executor.OutgoingWebHook
title: Outgoing WebHook
description: Sends an HTTP request to an external URL when executed. Supports GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE methods. For POST/PUT/PATCH, the source node is serialized as JSON for the request body. Custom headers and query parameters can be configured. The HTTP response body is stored in a target DataPoint's snapshot.
shortDescription: Sends an HTTP request to an external URL and stores the response in a target DataPoint.
llmPurpose: Sends an HTTP request to a configured URL with optional body, headers, and parameters, and stores the response in a target DataPoint.
llmBehavior: constructs HTTP request with URL, method, headers, query params
serializes source node as JSON body for POST/PUT/PATCH
sends HTTP request to external endpoint
stores response body in target DataPoint snapshot
llmOutputs: HTTP response stored in target DataPoint
llmTypicalUseCases: send sensor data to an external API
notify a Slack or Teams channel
post data to a cloud platform
llmPromptHints: send this data to an API
llmCreationHints: Create an OutgoingWebHook when the user wants to send data to an external HTTP endpoint. Requires a URL and method. Optionally configure source node for body, target DataPoint for response, headers, and query params.

llmConnectionHints.role: action node that sends HTTP requests and optionally stores responses

llmExamples: Send this sensor reading to my cloud API.
POST a notification to Slack when the temperature is high.
Call this REST endpoint with the latest data.
Send a GET request to check a remote service status.
